questions.explain: A nonzero discriminant means no cusp or node.

questions.q: A singular cubic typically has what local defect?
questions.explain: Singularity appears as node/cusp where derivatives vanish simultaneously.

questions.q: On an elliptic curve, $P+Q+R=\mathcal O$ when:
questions.explain: The secant line meets the cubic in three points summing to zero.

questions.q: The identity element for the elliptic-curve group law is:
questions.explain: Projectively, the neutral element is the point at infinity.

questions.q: If a point has order $5$, the smallest positive $n$ with $nP=\mathcal O$ is:
questions.explain: That is the definition of order.

questions.q: Two short Weierstrass curves over $\bar k$ are isomorphic iff:
choices: they have the same number of rational points over every finite field
the coefficients $(a,b)$ agree up to sign
questions.explain: $j(E)=1728\cdot 4a^3/(4a^3+27b^2)$ is a complete isomorphism invariant over $\bar k$.
With $b=0$, $j=1728\cdot 4a^3/(4a^3)=1728$. This is the CM-by-$\mathbb{Z}[i]$ curve.
At $j=0$ the curve has an order-$6$ automorphism (via $\omega$); at $j=1728$ it has an order-$4$ automorphism (via $i$). Generic $j$ gives $\operatorname{Aut}(E)=\{\pm1\}$.

questions.q: Over $\mathbb{C}$, every elliptic curve is analytically isomorphic to:
choices: a quotient $\mathbb{C}/\Lambda$ by a rank-$2$ lattice
questions.explain: The Weierstrass $\wp$-function embeds $\mathbb{C}/\Lambda$ as a cubic $y^2=4x^3-g_2 x-g_3$ in $\mathbb{CP}^2$.

questions.q: An elliptic curve $E/\mathbb{C}$ has complex multiplication when:
questions.explain: CM means $\operatorname{End}(E)$ is an order in an imaginary quadratic field $K$; generically $\operatorname{End}(E)=\mathbb{Z}$.

questions.q: For the CM lattice $\Lambda=\mathbb{Z}[i]$, the endomorphism 'multiplication by $i$' has degree (i.e. number of sheets as an isogeny) equal to $|i|^2=$?
questions.explain: $|i|^2=1$, so multiplication by $i$ is an automorphism (degree $1$), consistent with $j=1728$ having $\operatorname{Aut}(E)\supset\{\pm1,\pm i\}$.
Rank is the exponent of the free part.

questions.q: The torsion subgroup in Mordell–Weil is:
questions.explain: Over $\mathbb{Q}$, torsion is finite (Mazur classifies possibilities).
